description: deriver_no_memory_signal [M] -- Pattern-level test for the failure mode: the deriver writes observations that don't add signal a future agent could use about the user OR their work. Memory's job is to capture the user-and-their-work: (a) the user -- identity, traits, preferences, work patterns, recurring concerns; (b) the work -- what they're building, codebase, system architecture, stack/tools, problem domain, projects, active tasks. A failed observation adds neither. Discriminator (per-observation): would a future agent be better off with this in their context than without it? If the observation merely records a conversational event (an acknowledgment, task instruction, sentence-shaped one-off request) without conveying durable or current-state information about who the user is or what they're working on, it fails. Pure durability over-blocks legitimate transient-but-meaningful state ('user is currently debugging the payment migration' is transient but adds work signal). The dual-signal criterion (user OR work) handles both. ===== Relationship to existing categories ===== This is a broader umbrella for what Yuya's Deriver Judge calls META_CONVERSATIONAL (`evals/auxiliary/deriver/__main__.py:163`). Yuya's narrow definition targets conversation events ('X talked to Y', 'X met with Y'). This broader name covers (a) speech-act paraphrase ('X confirmed with yes', 'X said go'), (b) task instructions paraphrased as user attributes ('X wants to delete Y', 'X asked someone to do Z'), and (c) momentary-state-as-stable-preference ('X wants a setting flipped to false'). All fail the durability test. The connection to the molecular-facts framework (Gunjal & Durrett, EMNLP 2024, https://arxiv.org/abs/2406.20079): these observations may be decontextualized but they are non-durable -- properties an agent could rely on a week later. ===== Empirical evidence ===== Verified firing in production: 5 conclusions matching the pattern observed in the kassandra/claude_code workspace as of 2026-05-05. IDs AqhMhvQK1r11NbbrMGsjK ('kassandra confirmed something with yes'), 1fRIzuLAyOBN4kuS743Ct ('kassandra gave a confirmation to proceed with go on May 1, 2026'), YPXvAc_RKn8yDmcgbPPI5 ('kassandra wants a setting flipped to false'), me2dK53e7NLJ2pug3WPCM ('kassandra asked someone to review their linear issues and help plan their day on May 1, 2026'), wdqpQf8IUp8Q_ZqmJOsZO ('kassandra wants someone to look at her linear tasks and update them'). All five carry zero or trace-level signal about the user or their work. ===== Inputs ===== 10 real coding-agent sessions sampled from the SWE-chat dataset (SALT-NLP/SWE-chat, ODC-BY, https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.20779), balanced across user_persona (3 Expert Nitpicker, 3 Vague Requester, 3 Mind Changer, 1 Other). Selection criteria documented in tests/unified/test_cases/data/swe_chat_sample_persona_balanced.json. ===== Invariant ===== For each ingested session, the user's representation must NOT include any Tier 1 bare-speech-act / event-record observations (observations capturing the conversational EVENT of someone saying / confirming / doing something, without the substantive content). Tier 2 underspecified-attribute observations are NOT violations of this fixture's invariant -- they are a real failure mode but are tracked separately. Faithful records of user properties (identity, role, traits, preferences, patterns) and work context (codebase, system, stack, domain, projects, current focus with specificity) are NOT violations. ===== Aggregate metric ===== Leak rate = #(sessions where judge finds at least one no-signal observation) / 10. With continue_on_failure=true, all 10 sessions run; failures aggregate into a leak-rate signal in the runner output. ===== Tier scoping (added 2026-05-11) ===== After the first verify-first run on 2026-05-11 produced a 7/8 leak rate, the judge prompt was narrowed to fail ONLY on Tier 1 (bare-speech-act / event-record observations: 'user said yes', 'user pushed changes', 'user reviewed PR feedback', 'user stated "we are #169"', etc.). Tier 2 observations (underspecified-task-instruction shapes like 'user requested certain changes be tested' where the WHAT is missing) PASS this fixture's gate. Rationale: Tier 1 and Tier 2 are structurally different bugs that need different fixes (Tier 1 = stop writing bare events; Tier 2 = include the specific subject/object). Conflating them in one gate would let a Tier-1-only fix show as partial improvement without telling us which tier moved. Tier 2 is real and worth its own fixture later; this fixture is scoped to Tier 1. ===== Polarity status ===== INITIAL polarity pass_if=true (invariant test). Per the verify-first discipline, will flip to pass_if=false (verified-firing reproducer) and document the verified leak rate after the first complete run on current Honcho if the leak rate is non-zero. First partial run (2026-05-06 with fail-fast, durability-framed prompt) showed the bug fires on session 0 with multiple no-signal observations. ===== Trace artifacts ===== After each per-session llm_judge query, a save_artifact step dumps the user's representation to <artifact_dir>/<swe_N>_representation.json. Use these for offline classification of which observations carry no signal (which sub-shape — speech-act paraphrase, task-instruction-as-attribute, momentary-state-as-preference) and to compute signal-to-noise ratios that the binary judge alone cannot expose. ===== Trimmed input set (2026-05-11) ===== Sessions swe_0 and swe_9 from the underlying swe_chat_sample_persona_balanced.json extract are intentionally skipped because they have data-quality issues that would confound the no-signal measurement: swe_0 has 18 user / 2 assistant messages where the 'user' content reads as agent-authored Japanese refactoring plans (mislabeled role), and swe_9 has 8 user / 1 assistant messages (essentially monologue). Both are real SWE-chat sessions; both would test 'how does the deriver behave on misattributed agent output' rather than 'how does the deriver behave on user-authored messages,' which isn't what this fixture targets. The 8 retained sessions are 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8 (3 Expert Nitpicker, 3 Vague Requester, 2 Mind Changer; the single Other persona session was dropped along with swe_9).

assertions.prompt:
You are evaluating a Honcho user representation. Determine whether it contains any Tier 1 bare-speech-act / event-record observations. The bug being tested: memory writing "X happened" instead of "what is true about the user or their work."

The line is calibrated by these production examples (real Honcho output, annotated by the failure-mode owner):

=== Tier 1 — FAILS THE TEST (always-wrong; no specifics could rescue these) ===
- "kassandra confirmed something with 'yes'." — bare speech act, nothing to anchor
- "kassandra gave a confirmation to proceed with 'go' on May 1, 2026." — timestamp doesn't add signal
- "kassandra confirmed to delete items." — bare confirmation, WHAT-was-deleted isn't captured
- "kassandra confirmed a deletion request on May 1, 2026." — same shape
- "kassandra asked for the status of PR 44." — event-record of a question, purpose not captured
- "claude executed the command 'cd ~/claude-projects/honcho' successfully on May 5, 2026, at 22:27:25." — shell-history noise persisted as durable fact

=== Tier 2 — PASSES THIS TEST (shape-correct but underspecified; tracked by a DIFFERENT failure mode) ===
- "kassandra wants a setting flipped to false." — WHICH setting? specificity gap
- "kassandra wants someone to look at her linear tasks and update them." — WHAT tasks?
- "kassandra asked someone to review their linear issues and help plan their day on May 1, 2026." — generic, underspecified
- "kassandra instructed to delete sessions on May 1, 2026." — WHICH sessions?
- "kassandra is asking for help to update a document." — WHICH document?

=== Signal — PASSES (substantive memory, real value to a future agent) ===
- "kassandra's coworkers at Plastic Labs are Vince, Vineeth, Yuya, Courtland, Eri, Abigail, Rajat, Adavya, and Lily." — durable bio fact, enumerated
- "kassandra is working on a linear issue titled 'Bottom Up Test Case Design: ...'." — specific work context with title
- "kassandra believes the existing unified test runner is located at honcho/tests/unified." — specific work knowledge
- "kassandra wants Honcho to remember previous sessions at least at a high level and with awareness of when they happened." — substantive Honcho-design preference
- "kassandra wants to delete all tracked sessions except for excadrill, honcho, dedenne, prompt-evolver, tom-swe, wiki, test-case-development, and swe-chat-dataset, and those ending in timestamps." — specific decision with enumerated set
- "kassandra does not want to delete logs or artifacts." — scoped, durable preference
- "kassandra is working on a harness project." — named project IS signal even without elaboration
- "kassandra is confused because her Honcho plugin is disabled but still running, and this is not an issue of needing to close/restart." — observation of user state with diagnostic specifics
- "kassandra disabled her Honcho plugin via the CLI that pulls up, which was /mcp." — event-record but with specific tool and command

=== The Tier 1 / Signal line ===
Event-records can be SIGNAL if they pin down specifics — naming a tool, a command, a project, a file, a system. "kassandra disabled her Honcho plugin via the CLI /mcp" is Signal because it names the tool and the command. "kassandra confirmed something with 'yes'" is Tier 1 because there's nothing to anchor — no WHAT, no WHICH, no WHO else, no WHERE. The presence of a timestamp alone (e.g., "on May 1, 2026") does not save an otherwise-bare observation.

=== Decision rule ===
For each observation in the representation, ask: does it match a Tier 1 shape from the examples above (bare confirmation, bare question with no anchor, shell-history record, conversation event with no semantic anchor)? If yes, the test FAILS. If every observation either matches Tier 2 (underspecified-but-shape-correct, which is bad-but-tracked-elsewhere), Signal (substantive or specifics-anchored event), or some other non-Tier-1 shape, the test PASSES.

Answer passed=true if NO Tier 1 observation is present. Answer passed=false if ANY Tier 1 observation is present. In your reasoning, quote each observation you considered Tier 1 verbatim.

steps.description: Trace: representation for user_6 after swe_6 ingestion (Mind Changer)

assertions.prompt:
You are evaluating a Honcho user representation. Determine whether it contains any Tier 1 bare-speech-act / event-record observations. The bug being tested: memory writing "X happened" instead of "what is true about the user or their work."

The line is calibrated by these production examples (real Honcho output, annotated by the failure-mode owner):

=== Tier 1 — FAILS THE TEST (always-wrong; no specifics could rescue these) ===
- "kassandra confirmed something with 'yes'." — bare speech act, nothing to anchor
- "kassandra gave a confirmation to proceed with 'go' on May 1, 2026." — timestamp doesn't add signal
- "kassandra confirmed to delete items." — bare confirmation, WHAT-was-deleted isn't captured
- "kassandra confirmed a deletion request on May 1, 2026." — same shape
- "kassandra asked for the status of PR 44." — event-record of a question, purpose not captured
- "claude executed the command 'cd ~/claude-projects/honcho' successfully on May 5, 2026, at 22:27:25." — shell-history noise persisted as durable fact

=== Tier 2 — PASSES THIS TEST (shape-correct but underspecified; tracked by a DIFFERENT failure mode) ===
- "kassandra wants a setting flipped to false." — WHICH setting? specificity gap
- "kassandra wants someone to look at her linear tasks and update them." — WHAT tasks?
- "kassandra asked someone to review their linear issues and help plan their day on May 1, 2026." — generic, underspecified
- "kassandra instructed to delete sessions on May 1, 2026." — WHICH sessions?
- "kassandra is asking for help to update a document." — WHICH document?

=== Signal — PASSES (substantive memory, real value to a future agent) ===
- "kassandra's coworkers at Plastic Labs are Vince, Vineeth, Yuya, Courtland, Eri, Abigail, Rajat, Adavya, and Lily." — durable bio fact, enumerated
- "kassandra is working on a linear issue titled 'Bottom Up Test Case Design: ...'." — specific work context with title
- "kassandra believes the existing unified test runner is located at honcho/tests/unified." — specific work knowledge
- "kassandra wants Honcho to remember previous sessions at least at a high level and with awareness of when they happened." — substantive Honcho-design preference
- "kassandra wants to delete all tracked sessions except for excadrill, honcho, dedenne, prompt-evolver, tom-swe, wiki, test-case-development, and swe-chat-dataset, and those ending in timestamps." — specific decision with enumerated set
- "kassandra does not want to delete logs or artifacts." — scoped, durable preference
- "kassandra is working on a harness project." — named project IS signal even without elaboration
- "kassandra is confused because her Honcho plugin is disabled but still running, and this is not an issue of needing to close/restart." — observation of user state with diagnostic specifics
- "kassandra disabled her Honcho plugin via the CLI that pulls up, which was /mcp." — event-record but with specific tool and command

=== The Tier 1 / Signal line ===
Event-records can be SIGNAL if they pin down specifics — naming a tool, a command, a project, a file, a system. "kassandra disabled her Honcho plugin via the CLI /mcp" is Signal because it names the tool and the command. "kassandra confirmed something with 'yes'" is Tier 1 because there's nothing to anchor — no WHAT, no WHICH, no WHO else, no WHERE. The presence of a timestamp alone (e.g., "on May 1, 2026") does not save an otherwise-bare observation.

=== Decision rule ===
For each observation in the representation, ask: does it match a Tier 1 shape from the examples above (bare confirmation, bare question with no anchor, shell-history record, conversation event with no semantic anchor)? If yes, the test FAILS. If every observation either matches Tier 2 (underspecified-but-shape-correct, which is bad-but-tracked-elsewhere), Signal (substantive or specifics-anchored event), or some other non-Tier-1 shape, the test PASSES.

Answer passed=true if NO Tier 1 observation is present. Answer passed=false if ANY Tier 1 observation is present. In your reasoning, quote each observation you considered Tier 1 verbatim.
